data.title: SoftBank acknowledges Stargate project requires more time
data.description:
SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) said it is taking longer than expected to get the Stargate project off the ground, acknowledging for the first time that the $500B AI collaboration in the U.S. with OpenAI is slowing down, 

SoftBank's CFO Yoshimitsu Goto said during an earnings briefing that all preparations are taking place at the same time. "Although it may be taking a little bit more time than we originally expected, but we [will] make sure that we will be able to make good progress on this," Goto added.

Goto added that the project progress of Stargate has been going slower than expected. "That was my feeling, my personal observation. One of the reasons is site selection. Of course, there are a lot of options, and it's been taking time to select a good site. And there are a lot of stakeholders. To build consensus, we need to have a lot of discussions and also technical issues and construction issues. There are a lot of things that we need to look at," said Goto

The company is trying to reach a consensus with partners, including Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) and Abu Dhabi’s MGX. The Japanese company expects to start “concrete” negotiations for its first Stargate datacenter site soon, according to Goto, Bloomberg News reported.

“We need to take our time to prepare a model case for Stargate,” Goto said to the analysts and reporters.

SoftBank's Founder Masayoshi Son and OpenAI Co-Founder Sam Altman introduced the Stargate project in January along with President Donald Trump, with promises to start deploying $100B “immediately” and raise that to about $500B. However, months later, it’s yet to start raising funds or building data centers.

Some of the discussions behind Stargate have slowed due to market volatility, uncertainty about U.S. trade policy and questions around the financial valuations of AI hardware, Bloomberg News reported in May. Previously, it was also reported that SoftBank had pitched the idea of taking part in a $1T AI manufacturing hub in Arizona to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and others.

OpenAI is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT).

Update: The story was updated with a statement from SoftBank.

data.title: NCR Voyix outlines $2.575B-$2.65B revenue outlook while advancing cloud and payments integration
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: NCR Voyix Corporation (VYX) Q2 2025

Management View

  • James G. Kelly, CEO, stated the company continued to execute on key initiatives, highlighting new customer wins in restaurant and retail, expanded adoption of the Voyix Commerce Platform (VCP), and growing interest in new cloud products and payment capabilities. He said, "We will be launching additional VCP capabilities beginning in the fourth quarter and continuing into next year. In retail, we will be launching our enterprise grocery and convenience point-of-sale, self-checkout and fuel at the NRF show in January. In restaurants, we'll be launching a new all-in-one application for labor, inventory, reporting and scheduling in the fourth quarter and our new centralized menu management solution in early 2026."
  • Kelly described the payments strategy: "We completed our pilot for Voyix Pay in the U.S. in July and are on track to complete the migration of our existing SME portfolio and sign new customers directly to the processing platform by mid-September." He also emphasized expanded acquiring capabilities in the U.K., Canada, and Latin America, enabled by Global Payments' acquisition of Worldpay.
  • On hardware, the CEO reported that "there have been no material changes to the tariff-related cost to our business, which we continue to estimate to be between $8 million and $12 million for the year."
  • Nick East, Chief Product Officer, provided product performance updates, noting "nearly 78,000 sites connected to the Voyix Commerce Platform, an increase of 16% year-over-year." He said, "In the first half of 2025, volumes were more than 50% higher than the prior year, and the VCP processed more than 500 million transaction API calls in June alone."
  • Beimnet Tadele, President of Restaurants, said, "In the second quarter, our restaurant business signed more than 200 new software and services customers... Software ARR increased 4% and total ARR increased 3% in the quarter."
  • Darren Wilson, President of Retail, reported, "Our retail business signed nearly 50 software and service customers. Our platform and payment sites increased 25% and 13%, respectively. Software ARR increased 9% and total ARR increased 6% in the quarter."
  • Brian J. Webb-Walsh, CFO, stated, "For the quarter, we delivered revenue and adjusted EBITDA in line with expectations. Total revenue of $666 million declined 8% due to continued softness in hardware sales. Recurring revenue increased 4% to $422 million and as a percent of total revenue improved over 700 basis points to 63%."

Outlook

  • Webb-Walsh reiterated, "We continue to expect revenue to range from $2.575 billion to $2.65 billion and adjusted EBITDA to range from $420 million to $445 million. Non-GAAP diluted EPS is expected to be between $0.75 and $0.80, and adjusted free cash flow is expected to be between $170 million and $190 million."
  • Kelly confirmed, "Since our call in May, there have been no material changes to the tariff-related cost to our business, which we continue to estimate to be between $8 million and $12 million for the year."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for the quarter was $666 million, with recurring revenue at $422 million, representing 63% of total revenue. Adjusted EBITDA reached $95 million, and margin expanded to 14.3%.
  • Platform sites increased 16% to 78,000, while payment sites rose 3% to 8,400.
  • Restaurant segment recurring revenue was $143 million, and total segment revenue was $205 million. Segment adjusted EBITDA was $68 million with a margin of 33.2%.
  • Retail segment recurring revenue was $277 million. Total segment revenue was $454 million. Segment adjusted EBITDA was $81 million, and margin was 17.8%.
  • Adjusted free cash flow was $37 million for the quarter before considering restructuring, tax, and product investments. CapEx was $42 million for the quarter.

Q&A

  • Daniel Rock Perlin, RBC Capital Markets: Asked about demand environment and visibility into free cash flow ramp in the second half. Kelly responded, "I would say we have not seen any pullback from customers... So I don't see necessarily a slowdown in terms of what they're looking to do to modernize their infrastructure." Webb-Walsh added, "Q2 was in line with our expectations on free cash flow. And to your point, we tend to generate our free cash flow in the second half."
  • Isaac Arthur Sellhausen, Oppenheimer: Inquired about restaurant EBITDA margin strength and tariff exposure. Webb-Walsh replied, "Good software and services growth and recurring revenue growth in restaurants is helping margin... We see restaurants finishing the year about 32%." On tariffs, Kelly stated, "We feel comfortable in the range that we just outlined... this is not going to end sometime soon."
  • William Alfred Nance, Goldman Sachs: Asked about cost initiatives and a contract renewal. Webb-Walsh shared, "Our cost program for this year, we sized it at $100 million, 2/3 is around vendor spend, 1/3 around our own labor. That program is largely executed or being executed. About 40% of the savings hit in the first half and about 60% will hit in the second half."
  • Kartik Mehta, Northcoast Research: Asked about payments and competition in restaurants. Kelly explained, "We're not on that system until, I guess, the fourth quarter... I would see it ramping into next year."
  • John Patrick McShane, Stifel: Asked about ARR trends and payment growth. Kelly noted, "ARR -- software ARR was something around $700 million. It's approaching $800 million today."
  • Alexander Blake Neumann, Stephens: Sought color on ARR backlog and forward software growth. Kelly and Wilson both indicated positive momentum and a shift towards recurring revenue.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a generally neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on execution, demand environment, cost control, and future visibility. Their primary concerns centered on the ramp in free cash flow, margin performance, and competitive positioning in payments.
  • Management's tone was confident, emphasizing progress on platform adoption, payments integration, and cost actions. Kelly used phrases like "I remain encouraged by the progress" and "I'm increasingly convinced that NCR Voyix can advance the foundational strengths."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management sustained a similar level of engagement and cautious optimism, with a continued focus on cost controls, margin improvement, and product innovation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for revenue, adjusted EBITDA, non-GAAP diluted EPS, and adjusted free cash flow remains unchanged from the previous quarter.
  • Recurring revenue and ARR growth rates improved, with platform and payment site gains accelerating in both restaurant and retail segments.
  • Management's confidence in product innovation, payments integration, and global service expansion is more pronounced, with increased detail on forthcoming launches and customer wins.
  • Analyst focus remained on execution, margin delivery, cash flow, and payment strategy, consistent with the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff exposure remains a key challenge, with management maintaining the estimated cost range of $8 million to $12 million and monitoring the situation closely.
  • Hardware sales softness continues to impact total revenue, though recurring revenue and platform growth offset some effects.
  • Analyst questions highlighted the need for sustained cost controls, execution on payment integration, and visible progress on software ARR and platform migration.

Final Takeaway

NCR Voyix’s management emphasized ongoing momentum in platform adoption, payments integration, and software-driven recurring revenue, while reiterating unchanged full-year guidance. Expansion of cloud-native applications, enhanced payment capabilities, and a disciplined approach to cost control are positioned as key drivers for future growth, with management expressing confidence in improved performance and customer engagement across core business segments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Which under-the-radar winners have the best chance for further gains?
data.description:
Looking for smaller stocks that have attracted strong investor interest so far in 2025, but are still positioned for further gains?

The list below is meant to focus on those names. It contains stocks with a market cap of $1B or less and have climbed at least 50% so far in 2025. All the names also have Buy or Strong Buy ratings from both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts. 

The stocks on the list are then ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. This system of grading stocks is based on their relative performance on key quantitative measures, like valuation, profitability and momentum.

Here is the list, with each stock listed along with its Quant Rating and year-to-date performance:

  • Innovative Solutions and Support (NASDAQ:ISSC) Quant Rating: 4.98; YTD Performance: +104.22%
  • Atai Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ATAI) Quant Rating: 4.98; YTD Performance: +211.28%

data.title: Arhaus soars after 'resilient high-end customers' support sales growth
data.description:
Arhaus (NASDAQ:ARHS) was one of the biggest gainers in the consumer sector in Thursday morning trading after the retailer topped expectations with its second quarter earnings report.

Revenue was up 15.7% year-over-year to a record level for Q2 of $358 million. Comparable growth was 10.5%, driven by the successful conversion of strong first-quarter demand, while demand comparable growth was -3.6%, reflecting macroeconomic volatility and external headwinds.

"We successfully brought operations of our Dallas Distribution Center in-house which ramped ahead of schedule. This transition enabled us to convert strong first quarter demand into net revenue more efficiently, and at a higher volume, than expected," highlighted CEO John Reed. "With a resilient high-end client base, a debt-free balance sheet, and a clear strategic plan, we are navigating the current environment from a position of strength," he added.

During the quarter, the company completed 3 showroom projects, including 2 relocations and 1 renovation.

Arhaus (NASDAQ:ARHS) ended the quarter with no long-term debt and a cash position of $235 million.

Looking ahead, Arhaus (ARHS) noted that demand comparable growth in July was up 15.7%, reflecting strong client engagement and the strength of the Arhaus product assortment. Full-year revenue of $1.29 billion to $1.38 billion is anticipated, and adjusted EBITDA is seen landing in a range of $123 million to $145 million.

Shares of Arhaus (ARHS) were up 21.1% in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: ABNB, DKNG, LLY, PTON, DUO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Thursday as investors weighed President Donald Trump's newly announced 100% tariff on imported semiconductors, exempting companies manufacturing in the U.S.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG) +7% – Shares jumped after the company reported mixed Q2 results but delivered upbeat guidance. Revenue rose 37.3% Y/Y, while monthly unique paying customers increased 6% to 3.3M. Average revenue per user climbed 29%, driven by higher sportsbook hold and improved promotional efficiency. DraftKings posted record adjusted EBITDA of $301M vs. $243.8M consensus. The company reaffirmed FY2025 revenue guidance of $6.2B–$6.4B (midpoint: $6.3B), with expectations leaning toward the higher end as it eyes a new state launch.
  • Peloton (PTON) +10% – Shares surged after the company reported only its second quarterly profit since 2021, fueled by deep cost cuts across marketing, R&D, and overall operations that offset continued revenue and subscriber declines. Despite a 6% drop in Q4 revenue to $606.9M and falling connected fitness subscriptions, results topped expectations. However, Peloton guided for further revenue and subscriber declines in the current quarter and FY2026 while also launching a new restructuring plan targeting $100M in cost savings.
  • Duolingo (DUOL) +33% – Shares surged after the language-learning platform raised its full-year guidance on the back of strong user growth fueled by AI integration. Revenue rose 41% Y/Y to $252M, beating the $241M consensus, while net income jumped 84% to $45M, or $0.91 per share. Daily active users climbed 40% Y/Y to nearly 48M. The company now expects Q3 revenue of $257M–$261M vs. $253.34M estimated and lifted its FY revenue forecast to $1.01B–$1.02B (prior: $987M–$996M) vs. the consensus of $996.50M, with bookings seen at $1.15B–$1.16B.

Biggest stock losers

  • Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB) -6% – Shares dipped after the company’s conservative Q3 outlook overshadowed upbeat Q2 results. While Q3 revenue is projected to grow 8–10% to $4.02B–$4.10B, aligning with the $4.05B consensus, the pace marks a slowdown from 13% growth in Q2. Airbnb expects over $2B in adjusted EBITDA for the full year, but at a lower margin of 34.5% due to increased spending on growth and policy initiatives. The company also flagged similar Y/Y margin declines for Q4 and expects Q3 nights and seats booked to stay “relatively stable” Q/Q.
  • Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) -4% – Shares came under pressure following mixed Q2 results, as revenue, despite rising 11% Y/Y, narrowly missed expectations. Gross bookings hit a record $4.5B, marking the 10th straight quarter of new highs, while active riders posted a ninth consecutive quarter of double-digit growth. For the current quarter, Lyft guided for gross bookings of $4.65B–$4.8B (above estimates), adjusted EBITDA of $125M–$145M, and EBITDA margin of 2.7%–3.0%.
  • e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) -11% –  Shares dropped after the cosmetics company reported Q2 results and issued cautious guidance for the first half of the fiscal year. The company now expects adjusted EBITDA margins of around 20%, down from approximately 23% in the prior-year period. Management declined to provide a full-year fiscal 2026 outlook, citing uncertainty around tariffs and a wide range of possible outcomes.
  • Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) -13% – Shares dropped sharply after the company released topline results from a Phase 3 trial of its oral obesity drug orforglipron. Despite hitting all primary and key secondary endpoints, investors appeared underwhelmed by the weight loss data, especially relative to high market expectations. In contrast, rival Novo Nordisk (NVO) +11% rallied as the market interpreted the data as less threatening to its dominant position in the GLP-1 obesity drug space. Lilly also reported strong Q2 results and raised its full-year guidance, but the orforglipron news overshadowed the earnings beat.
